{{translated page|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|version=107497098|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja
/ Tenman Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Kitanoten-jinja (Tokorozawa), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}} 3-28-44 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|26.0|N|139|25|43.3|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitano Tenjinsha}} |Deities = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine 2 / Minor Shrine 1 {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}})
{{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = Kitano Tenjinsha (collective name) |Annual festival = {{jalink|March 21|Q2433}} (Spring Grand Festival) |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Kitano Tenjinsha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[File:Kitano-Tenjin-sha sandō.jpg|thumb|At the southern approach of Kitano Tenjinsha]] '''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (きたのてんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (two proposed identifications, one {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}}), and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. The registered {{jalink|corporate registry|Q83708009}} religious corporation name is '''Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha''', and “Kitano Tenjinsha” is the collective name for these shrines.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha (Kitano) |date=2008-10-31 |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} === Enshrined deities === * Mononobe Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “Mononobe Tenjinsha”; jointly enshrines {{jalink|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|Izumo Iwai Shrine}}. * Kuniichi Chigi Jinja: {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “{{jalink|Kuniichi Chigi Shrine (disambiguation)||Kuniichi Chigi Shrine}}”. * Tenman Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} === Jointly enshrined === * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} * Kotesashi Myōjin * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}Summary displayed in front of the shrine, Kitano Tenjinsha. Viewed January 15, 2022. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded when {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} established the shrine during his eastern campaign. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received a {{jalink|shuinjō|Q11093673}} from the {{jalink|Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662}}. * Mononobe Tenjinsha was the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} (clan tutelary deity) of the Mononobe clan (see {{jalink|Nashiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Clan|Q11504612}}). The ancestral deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} is still enshrined today. * As a Tenjin shrine, in {{jalink|Chōtoku|Q1327213}} 1 ({{jalink|995|Q23794}}), {{jalink|Sugawara no Sukenari}}, a fifth-generation descendant of Sugawara no Michizane, invited the deity from Kyoto’s {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kitano Shrine}} while serving as {{jalink|Governor of Musashi}}. Thereafter, shrine buildings were constructed by {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}}, {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}}. * Edo public exhibition – From April 1 to June 1, {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 7 ({{jalink|1722|Q6918}}), a public display of the shrine’s sacred object was held at the former {{jalink|Mejiro Fudō-in Hasedera|Q11646320}} in {{jalink|Edo|Q215646}}. During this period, on April 24, hanging scrolls and documents from the shrine treasury were submitted inside Edo Castle at the shogun’s request.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hm9k-ajm/musasinorekisi/gosikifudoutomusasino/mejirofudou/kitanotennjinndekaityou/kitanotennjinnsyadekaityou.htm |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Edo Public Exhibition |work=Otamajakushi |date=2001-11-11 |publisher=Kiichi Ajima |accessdate=2015-06}} === Shrine grounds === Approaches lined with {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates and {{jalink|stone lanterns}} run along the western and southern sides of the grounds. The western side faces the former {{jalink|Kamakura Highway|Q2389309}}, which runs north–south, while from the south along {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 179 / Tokyo Metropolitan Route 179|Q11427110}} one reaches the worship hall via multiple {{jalink|stairways|Q12511}} and torii gates. ; Subsidiary shrines * Inari Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q124483190}} – Founded on September 25, {{jalink|1937|Q24666}} at {{jalink|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|Q11658682}} at the wish of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|Q5694703}}. It was established to enshrine the spirits of fallen aviation personnel who had devoted their lives to the development of national aviation but were not enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. In 1938, with the founding of the {{jalink|Army Aviation School|Q2822344}}, it was transferred from Tokorozawa to Toyooka (present-day {{jalink|Iruma Air Base|Q3015843}}). On September 3, 1945, before the site was requisitioned by U.S. forces, it was relocated to the grounds of this shrine. * {{jalink|Ayako Tenmangū|Q17191862|Ayako Tenjinsha}} – Enshrines {{jalink|Tajihi no Ayako}}, the {{jalink|wet nurse|Q472898}} of {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. * Kotesashi Shrine – Enshrines the war dead (heroic spirits) of the Kotesashi area. Kitano Tenjin Inari.jpg|Inari Shrine Kitano Tenjin Yagumo.jpg|Yakumo Shrine KitanoTenjin AyakoJinja.jpg|Ayako Tenjinsha Kotesashi-jinja 14.jpg|Kotesashi Shrine / Aviation Shrine ; Trees on the grounds * Mikoto-zakura – A {{jalink|cherry tree|Q19939}} said to have been planted by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} in front of the shrine; it has repeatedly died and regenerated through {{jalink|suckers|Q960522}}. * Dainagon Plum – Planted by {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}} in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 18 ({{jalink|1590|Q6764}}); the current tree (as of 2015) is the second generation. Claiming descent from Sugawara no Michizane and using the {{jalink|plum crest}}, Maeda Toshiie is said to have stopped at the then-dilapidated shrine during the {{jalink|Odawara Campaign|Q2622890}} and rebuilt the shrine buildings, planting a single plum tree. The name derives from his court rank of {{jalink|Dainagon|Q1354706}} at the time. ; Other features * Kitano Place-Name Origin Monument – A stone monument erected on September 25, {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 18 ({{jalink|2006|Q2021}}), recording that although recent land readjustment changed the shrine’s address to Kotesashi Motomachi, the surrounding place-name “Kitano” derives from this shrine. * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} encampment site * {{jalink|Temizuya}} – Rebuilt in recent years ({{jalink|2010s|Q19022}}), with a {{jalink|dragon|Q11675}} {{jalink|ceiling painting}}. * Kagura hall * {{jalink|Chikaraishi|Q1660393}} * {{jalink|Sacred horse|Q11591047}} (shinme) Dainagon-ume Kitano-Tenjin-sha.jpg|Dainagon Plum Kitano Tenjin shinme.jpg|Sacred horse === Cultural properties === === Saitama Prefecture–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Kitano Tenjin Engi{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/kenshiteibunkazai/bunzai_20100510170056823.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Engi, with seven former roller ends |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Painting) – Seven hanging scrolls on silk with color, with seven former roller ends === Tokorozawa City–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Monument inscribed with a poem on the Mikoto-zakura{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/meisyo/bunzai_20100601150043467.html |title=Poem Monument of the Mikoto-zakura |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Place of scenic beauty) – Erected in {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 2 ({{jalink|1802|Q6883}}); the oldest poem monument in the city. ** Medieval documents of Kitano Tenjinsha{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/komonjo/bunzai_20100810104554996.html |title=Medieval Documents of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Ancient documents) – Eleven documents including a land donation charter by {{jalink|Ashikaga Ujimitsu|Q4306934}}. === Affiliated shrines === * {{jalink|Fujimori Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Fujimori Inari Shrine}} * {{jalink|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Rokusho Shrine}} * {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Suwa Shrine}} – {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}} Kotesashi Minami 1-17-21 Suwa Jinja Kotesashi.jpg|Suwa Shrine === Annual festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – Prayer amulets are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsumōde|Q1032381}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * {{jalink|Setsubun Festival}} ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – Corresponds to the shrine’s Great Purification; “Shikinaisha Kitano Shrine Great Purification Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsuuma|Q510847}} Festival ({{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}) – Held at the Inari Shrine; “Inari Shrine Amulets” are distributed. * Prayer for Harvest Festival ({{jalink|February 27|Q2361}}) – Also called Toshigoi-no-matsuri; “Prayer for Harvest Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|March 21|Q2433}}) – Paired with the Autumn Grand Festival and regarded as the most important festival of the shrine; “Kuniichi Chigi Shrine Good Fortune and Protection Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Festival ({{jalink|March 25|Q2454}}) – Also called Niban-machi; “Mononobe Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Sericulture|Q11236}} Festival ({{jalink|April 3|Q2509}}) – Held at the Ayako Tenjinsha; “Ayako Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * Memorial Service ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) – Held at the Kotesashi Shrine to console the spirits of local war dead since the Sino-Japanese and Russo-Japanese wars. * Yakumo Festival – Eve festival on {{jalink|July 14|Q2691}}, main festival on {{jalink|July 15|Q2686}}; “Yakumo Shrine Disaster-Prevention Amulets” are distributed. * Autumn Grand Festival (late September) – Hayashi music is offered every year, and Satokagura is offered every two years. * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) – “Niiname Festival Amulets” are distributed.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kitanotenjin.com/nenkangyouji.html |title=Festivals of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Kitano Tenjinsha |accessdate=2022-01-15}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Tokorozawa City History Research, No. 3: “On the Kitano Tenjin Engi Picture Scrolls” (Author: Nakamichi Kurihara) === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District Part 3|chapter=Kitano Village, Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} * [https://kitanotenjin.com/ Kitano Tenjinsha] (official shrine website) * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha (Kitano Tenjinsha) |url=https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8910/ |publisher=Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2018-03-12}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Kitano Tenjinsha |url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |publisher=Tokorozawa City |date=2015-06-23 |accessdate=2017-10-15}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:所沢市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]

Shrine|WD=Q135098982|1=Kuniwichino- Shrine}} ==References== [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kitano Tenjinsha]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] {{commons category|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} [[JA:北野天神社 (所沢市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11404276}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|version=107497098|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja
/ Tenman Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Kitanoten-jinja (Tokorozawa), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}} 3-28-44 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|26.0|N|139|25|43.3|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitano Tenjinsha}} |Deities = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine 2 / Minor Shrine 1 {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}})
{{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = Kitano Tenjinsha (collective name) |Annual festival = {{jalink|March 21|Q2433}} (Spring Grand Festival) |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Kitano Tenjinsha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[File:Kitano-Tenjin-sha sandō.jpg|thumb|At the southern approach of Kitano Tenjinsha]] '''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (きたのてんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (two proposed identifications, one {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}}), and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. The registered {{jalink|corporate registry|Q83708009}} religious corporation name is '''Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha''', and “Kitano Tenjinsha” is the collective name for these shrines.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha (Kitano) |date=2008-10-31 |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} === Enshrined deities === * Mononobe Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “Mononobe Tenjinsha”; jointly enshrines {{jalink|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|Izumo Iwai Shrine}}. * Kuniichi Chigi Jinja: {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “{{jalink|Kuniichi Chigi Shrine (disambiguation)||Kuniichi Chigi Shrine}}”. * Tenman Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} === Jointly enshrined === * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} * Kotesashi Myōjin * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}Summary displayed in front of the shrine, Kitano Tenjinsha. Viewed January 15, 2022. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded when {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} established the shrine during his eastern campaign. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received a {{jalink|shuinjō|Q11093673}} from the {{jalink|Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662}}. * Mononobe Tenjinsha was the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} (clan tutelary deity) of the Mononobe clan (see {{jalink|Nashiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Clan|Q11504612}}). The ancestral deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} is still enshrined today. * As a Tenjin shrine, in {{jalink|Chōtoku|Q1327213}} 1 ({{jalink|995|Q23794}}), {{jalink|Sugawara no Sukenari}}, a fifth-generation descendant of Sugawara no Michizane, invited the deity from Kyoto’s {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kitano Shrine}} while serving as {{jalink|Governor of Musashi}}. Thereafter, shrine buildings were constructed by {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}}, {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}}. * Edo public exhibition – From April 1 to June 1, {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 7 ({{jalink|1722|Q6918}}), a public display of the shrine’s sacred object was held at the former {{jalink|Mejiro Fudō-in Hasedera|Q11646320}} in {{jalink|Edo|Q215646}}. During this period, on April 24, hanging scrolls and documents from the shrine treasury were submitted inside Edo Castle at the shogun’s request.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hm9k-ajm/musasinorekisi/gosikifudoutomusasino/mejirofudou/kitanotennjinndekaityou/kitanotennjinnsyadekaityou.htm |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Edo Public Exhibition |work=Otamajakushi |date=2001-11-11 |publisher=Kiichi Ajima |accessdate=2015-06}} === Shrine grounds === Approaches lined with {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates and {{jalink|stone lanterns}} run along the western and southern sides of the grounds. The western side faces the former {{jalink|Kamakura Highway|Q2389309}}, which runs north–south, while from the south along {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 179 / Tokyo Metropolitan Route 179|Q11427110}} one reaches the worship hall via multiple {{jalink|stairways|Q12511}} and torii gates. ; Subsidiary shrines * Inari Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q124483190}} – Founded on September 25, {{jalink|1937|Q24666}} at {{jalink|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|Q11658682}} at the wish of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|Q5694703}}. It was established to enshrine the spirits of fallen aviation personnel who had devoted their lives to the development of national aviation but were not enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. In 1938, with the founding of the {{jalink|Army Aviation School|Q2822344}}, it was transferred from Tokorozawa to Toyooka (present-day {{jalink|Iruma Air Base|Q3015843}}). On September 3, 1945, before the site was requisitioned by U.S. forces, it was relocated to the grounds of this shrine. * {{jalink|Ayako Tenmangū|Q17191862|Ayako Tenjinsha}} – Enshrines {{jalink|Tajihi no Ayako}}, the {{jalink|wet nurse|Q472898}} of {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. * Kotesashi Shrine – Enshrines the war dead (heroic spirits) of the Kotesashi area. Kitano Tenjin Inari.jpg|Inari Shrine Kitano Tenjin Yagumo.jpg|Yakumo Shrine KitanoTenjin AyakoJinja.jpg|Ayako Tenjinsha Kotesashi-jinja 14.jpg|Kotesashi Shrine / Aviation Shrine ; Trees on the grounds * Mikoto-zakura – A {{jalink|cherry tree|Q19939}} said to have been planted by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} in front of the shrine; it has repeatedly died and regenerated through {{jalink|suckers|Q960522}}. * Dainagon Plum – Planted by {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}} in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 18 ({{jalink|1590|Q6764}}); the current tree (as of 2015) is the second generation. Claiming descent from Sugawara no Michizane and using the {{jalink|plum crest}}, Maeda Toshiie is said to have stopped at the then-dilapidated shrine during the {{jalink|Odawara Campaign|Q2622890}} and rebuilt the shrine buildings, planting a single plum tree. The name derives from his court rank of {{jalink|Dainagon|Q1354706}} at the time. ; Other features * Kitano Place-Name Origin Monument – A stone monument erected on September 25, {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 18 ({{jalink|2006|Q2021}}), recording that although recent land readjustment changed the shrine’s address to Kotesashi Motomachi, the surrounding place-name “Kitano” derives from this shrine. * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} encampment site * {{jalink|Temizuya}} – Rebuilt in recent years ({{jalink|2010s|Q19022}}), with a {{jalink|dragon|Q11675}} {{jalink|ceiling painting}}. * Kagura hall * {{jalink|Chikaraishi|Q1660393}} * {{jalink|Sacred horse|Q11591047}} (shinme) Dainagon-ume Kitano-Tenjin-sha.jpg|Dainagon Plum Kitano Tenjin shinme.jpg|Sacred horse === Cultural properties === === Saitama Prefecture–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Kitano Tenjin Engi{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/kenshiteibunkazai/bunzai_20100510170056823.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Engi, with seven former roller ends |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Painting) – Seven hanging scrolls on silk with color, with seven former roller ends === Tokorozawa City–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Monument inscribed with a poem on the Mikoto-zakura{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/meisyo/bunzai_20100601150043467.html |title=Poem Monument of the Mikoto-zakura |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Place of scenic beauty) – Erected in {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 2 ({{jalink|1802|Q6883}}); the oldest poem monument in the city. ** Medieval documents of Kitano Tenjinsha{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/komonjo/bunzai_20100810104554996.html |title=Medieval Documents of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Ancient documents) – Eleven documents including a land donation charter by {{jalink|Ashikaga Ujimitsu|Q4306934}}. === Affiliated shrines === * {{jalink|Fujimori Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Fujimori Inari Shrine}} * {{jalink|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Rokusho Shrine}} * {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Suwa Shrine}} – {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}} Kotesashi Minami 1-17-21 Suwa Jinja Kotesashi.jpg|Suwa Shrine === Annual festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – Prayer amulets are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsumōde|Q1032381}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * {{jalink|Setsubun Festival}} ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – Corresponds to the shrine’s Great Purification; “Shikinaisha Kitano Shrine Great Purification Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsuuma|Q510847}} Festival ({{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}) – Held at the Inari Shrine; “Inari Shrine Amulets” are distributed. * Prayer for Harvest Festival ({{jalink|February 27|Q2361}}) – Also called Toshigoi-no-matsuri; “Prayer for Harvest Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|March 21|Q2433}}) – Paired with the Autumn Grand Festival and regarded as the most important festival of the shrine; “Kuniichi Chigi Shrine Good Fortune and Protection Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Festival ({{jalink|March 25|Q2454}}) – Also called Niban-machi; “Mononobe Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Sericulture|Q11236}} Festival ({{jalink|April 3|Q2509}}) – Held at the Ayako Tenjinsha; “Ayako Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * Memorial Service ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) – Held at the Kotesashi Shrine to console the spirits of local war dead since the Sino-Japanese and Russo-Japanese wars. * Yakumo Festival – Eve festival on {{jalink|July 14|Q2691}}, main festival on {{jalink|July 15|Q2686}}; “Yakumo Shrine Disaster-Prevention Amulets” are distributed. * Autumn Grand Festival (late September) – Hayashi music is offered every year, and Satokagura is offered every two years. * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) – “Niiname Festival Amulets” are distributed.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kitanotenjin.com/nenkangyouji.html |title=Festivals of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Kitano Tenjinsha |accessdate=2022-01-15}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Tokorozawa City History Research, No. 3: “On the Kitano Tenjin Engi Picture Scrolls” (Author: Nakamichi Kurihara) === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District Part 3|chapter=Kitano Village, Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} * [https://kitanotenjin.com/ Kitano Tenjinsha] (official shrine website) * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha (Kitano Tenjinsha) |url=https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8910/ |publisher=Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2018-03-12}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Kitano Tenjinsha |url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |publisher=Tokorozawa City |date=2015-06-23 |accessdate=2017-10-15}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]

Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}} 3-28-44 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|26.0|N|139|25|43.3|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitano Tenjinsha}} |Deities = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine 2 / Minor Shrine 1 {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}})
{{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = Kitano Tenjinsha (collective name) |Annual festival = {{jalink|March 21|Q2433}} (Spring Grand Festival) |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Kitano Tenjinsha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[File:Kitano-Tenjin-sha sandō.jpg|thumb|At the southern approach of Kitano Tenjinsha]] '''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (きたのてんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (two proposed identifications, one {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}}), and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. The registered {{jalink|corporate registry|Q83708009}} religious corporation name is '''Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha''', and “Kitano Tenjinsha” is the collective name for these shrines.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha (Kitano) |date=2008-10-31 |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} === Enshrined deities === * Mononobe Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “Mononobe Tenjinsha”; jointly enshrines {{jalink|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|Izumo Iwai Shrine}}. * Kuniichi Chigi Jinja: {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “{{jalink|Kuniichi Chigi Shrine (disambiguation)||Kuniichi Chigi Shrine}}”. * Tenman Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} === Jointly enshrined === * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} * Kotesashi Myōjin * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}Summary displayed in front of the shrine, Kitano Tenjinsha. Viewed January 15, 2022. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded when {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} established the shrine during his eastern campaign. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received a {{jalink|shuinjō|Q11093673}} from the {{jalink|Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662}}. * Mononobe Tenjinsha was the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} (clan tutelary deity) of the Mononobe clan (see {{jalink|Nashiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Clan|Q11504612}}). The ancestral deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} is still enshrined today. * As a Tenjin shrine, in {{jalink|Chōtoku|Q1327213}} 1 ({{jalink|995|Q23794}}), {{jalink|Sugawara no Sukenari}}, a fifth-generation descendant of Sugawara no Michizane, invited the deity from Kyoto’s {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kitano Shrine}} while serving as {{jalink|Governor of Musashi}}. Thereafter, shrine buildings were constructed by {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}}, {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}}. * Edo public exhibition – From April 1 to June 1, {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 7 ({{jalink|1722|Q6918}}), a public display of the shrine’s sacred object was held at the former {{jalink|Mejiro Fudō-in Hasedera|Q11646320}} in {{jalink|Edo|Q215646}}. During this period, on April 24, hanging scrolls and documents from the shrine treasury were submitted inside Edo Castle at the shogun’s request.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hm9k-ajm/musasinorekisi/gosikifudoutomusasino/mejirofudou/kitanotennjinndekaityou/kitanotennjinnsyadekaityou.htm |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Edo Public Exhibition |work=Otamajakushi |date=2001-11-11 |publisher=Kiichi Ajima |accessdate=2015-06}} === Shrine grounds === Approaches lined with {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates and {{jalink|stone lanterns}} run along the western and southern sides of the grounds. The western side faces the former {{jalink|Kamakura Highway|Q2389309}}, which runs north–south, while from the south along {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 179 / Tokyo Metropolitan Route 179|Q11427110}} one reaches the worship hall via multiple {{jalink|stairways|Q12511}} and torii gates. ; Subsidiary shrines * Inari Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q124483190}} – Founded on September 25, {{jalink|1937|Q24666}} at {{jalink|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|Q11658682}} at the wish of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|Q5694703}}. It was established to enshrine the spirits of fallen aviation personnel who had devoted their lives to the development of national aviation but were not enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. In 1938, with the founding of the {{jalink|Army Aviation School|Q2822344}}, it was transferred from Tokorozawa to Toyooka (present-day {{jalink|Iruma Air Base|Q3015843}}). On September 3, 1945, before the site was requisitioned by U.S. forces, it was relocated to the grounds of this shrine. * {{jalink|Ayako Tenmangū|Q17191862|Ayako Tenjinsha}} – Enshrines {{jalink|Tajihi no Ayako}}, the {{jalink|wet nurse|Q472898}} of {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. * Kotesashi Shrine – Enshrines the war dead (heroic spirits) of the Kotesashi area. Kitano Tenjin Inari.jpg|Inari Shrine Kitano Tenjin Yagumo.jpg|Yakumo Shrine KitanoTenjin AyakoJinja.jpg|Ayako Tenjinsha Kotesashi-jinja 14.jpg|Kotesashi Shrine / Aviation Shrine ; Trees on the grounds * Mikoto-zakura – A {{jalink|cherry tree|Q19939}} said to have been planted by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} in front of the shrine; it has repeatedly died and regenerated through {{jalink|suckers|Q960522}}. * Dainagon Plum – Planted by {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}} in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 18 ({{jalink|1590|Q6764}}); the current tree (as of 2015) is the second generation. Claiming descent from Sugawara no Michizane and using the {{jalink|plum crest}}, Maeda Toshiie is said to have stopped at the then-dilapidated shrine during the {{jalink|Odawara Campaign|Q2622890}} and rebuilt the shrine buildings, planting a single plum tree. The name derives from his court rank of {{jalink|Dainagon|Q1354706}} at the time. ; Other features * Kitano Place-Name Origin Monument – A stone monument erected on September 25, {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 18 ({{jalink|2006|Q2021}}), recording that although recent land readjustment changed the shrine’s address to Kotesashi Motomachi, the surrounding place-name “Kitano” derives from this shrine. * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} encampment site * {{jalink|Temizuya}} – Rebuilt in recent years ({{jalink|2010s|Q19022}}), with a {{jalink|dragon|Q11675}} {{jalink|ceiling painting}}. * Kagura hall * {{jalink|Chikaraishi|Q1660393}} * {{jalink|Sacred horse|Q11591047}} (shinme) Dainagon-ume Kitano-Tenjin-sha.jpg|Dainagon Plum Kitano Tenjin shinme.jpg|Sacred horse === Cultural properties === === Saitama Prefecture–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Kitano Tenjin Engi{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/kenshiteibunkazai/bunzai_20100510170056823.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Engi, with seven former roller ends |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Painting) – Seven hanging scrolls on silk with color, with seven former roller ends === Tokorozawa City–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Monument inscribed with a poem on the Mikoto-zakura{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/meisyo/bunzai_20100601150043467.html |title=Poem Monument of the Mikoto-zakura |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Place of scenic beauty) – Erected in {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 2 ({{jalink|1802|Q6883}}); the oldest poem monument in the city. ** Medieval documents of Kitano Tenjinsha{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/komonjo/bunzai_20100810104554996.html |title=Medieval Documents of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Ancient documents) – Eleven documents including a land donation charter by {{jalink|Ashikaga Ujimitsu|Q4306934}}. === Affiliated shrines === * {{jalink|Fujimori Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Fujimori Inari Shrine}} * {{jalink|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Rokusho Shrine}} * {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Suwa Shrine}} – {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}} Kotesashi Minami 1-17-21 Suwa Jinja Kotesashi.jpg|Suwa Shrine === Annual festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – Prayer amulets are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsumōde|Q1032381}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * {{jalink|Setsubun Festival}} ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – Corresponds to the shrine’s Great Purification; “Shikinaisha Kitano Shrine Great Purification Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsuuma|Q510847}} Festival ({{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}) – Held at the Inari Shrine; “Inari Shrine Amulets” are distributed. * Prayer for Harvest Festival ({{jalink|February 27|Q2361}}) – Also called Toshigoi-no-matsuri; “Prayer for Harvest Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|March 21|Q2433}}) – Paired with the Autumn Grand Festival and regarded as the most important festival of the shrine; “Kuniichi Chigi Shrine Good Fortune and Protection Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Festival ({{jalink|March 25|Q2454}}) – Also called Niban-machi; “Mononobe Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Sericulture|Q11236}} Festival ({{jalink|April 3|Q2509}}) – Held at the Ayako Tenjinsha; “Ayako Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * Memorial Service ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) – Held at the Kotesashi Shrine to console the spirits of local war dead since the Sino-Japanese and Russo-Japanese wars. * Yakumo Festival – Eve festival on {{jalink|July 14|Q2691}}, main festival on {{jalink|July 15|Q2686}}; “Yakumo Shrine Disaster-Prevention Amulets” are distributed. * Autumn Grand Festival (late September) – Hayashi music is offered every year, and Satokagura is offered every two years. * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) – “Niiname Festival Amulets” are distributed.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kitanotenjin.com/nenkangyouji.html |title=Festivals of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Kitano Tenjinsha |accessdate=2022-01-15}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Tokorozawa City History Research, No. 3: “On the Kitano Tenjin Engi Picture Scrolls” (Author: Nakamichi Kurihara) === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District Part 3|chapter=Kitano Village, Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} * [https://kitanotenjin.com/ Kitano Tenjinsha] (official shrine website) * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha (Kitano Tenjinsha) |url=https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8910/ |publisher=Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2018-03-12}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Kitano Tenjinsha |url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |publisher=Tokorozawa City |date=2015-06-23 |accessdate=2017-10-15}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]

content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja
/ Tenman Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Kitanoten-jinja (Tokorozawa), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}} 3-28-44 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|26.0|N|139|25|43.3|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitano Tenjinsha}} |Deities = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine 2 / Minor Shrine 1 {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}})
{{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = Kitano Tenjinsha (collective name) |Annual festival = {{jalink|March 21|Q2433}} (Spring Grand Festival) |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Kitano Tenjinsha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[File:Kitano-Tenjin-sha sandō.jpg|thumb|At the southern approach of Kitano Tenjinsha]] '''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (きたのてんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (two proposed identifications, one {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}}), and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. The registered {{jalink|corporate registry|Q83708009}} religious corporation name is '''Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha''', and “Kitano Tenjinsha” is the collective name for these shrines.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha (Kitano) |date=2008-10-31 |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} === Enshrined deities === * Mononobe Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “Mononobe Tenjinsha”; jointly enshrines {{jalink|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|Izumo Iwai Shrine}}. * Kuniichi Chigi Jinja: {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “{{jalink|Kuniichi Chigi Shrine (disambiguation)||Kuniichi Chigi Shrine}}”. * Tenman Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} === Jointly enshrined === * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} * Kotesashi Myōjin * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}Summary displayed in front of the shrine, Kitano Tenjinsha. Viewed January 15, 2022. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded when {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} established the shrine during his eastern campaign. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received a {{jalink|shuinjō|Q11093673}} from the {{jalink|Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662}}. * Mononobe Tenjinsha was the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} (clan tutelary deity) of the Mononobe clan (see {{jalink|Nashiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Clan|Q11504612}}). The ancestral deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} is still enshrined today. * As a Tenjin shrine, in {{jalink|Chōtoku|Q1327213}} 1 ({{jalink|995|Q23794}}), {{jalink|Sugawara no Sukenari}}, a fifth-generation descendant of Sugawara no Michizane, invited the deity from Kyoto’s {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kitano Shrine}} while serving as {{jalink|Governor of Musashi}}. Thereafter, shrine buildings were constructed by {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}}, {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}}. * Edo public exhibition – From April 1 to June 1, {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 7 ({{jalink|1722|Q6918}}), a public display of the shrine’s sacred object was held at the former {{jalink|Mejiro Fudō-in Hasedera|Q11646320}} in {{jalink|Edo|Q215646}}. During this period, on April 24, hanging scrolls and documents from the shrine treasury were submitted inside Edo Castle at the shogun’s request.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hm9k-ajm/musasinorekisi/gosikifudoutomusasino/mejirofudou/kitanotennjinndekaityou/kitanotennjinnsyadekaityou.htm |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Edo Public Exhibition |work=Otamajakushi |date=2001-11-11 |publisher=Kiichi Ajima |accessdate=2015-06}} === Shrine grounds === Approaches lined with {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates and {{jalink|stone lanterns}} run along the western and southern sides of the grounds. The western side faces the former {{jalink|Kamakura Highway|Q2389309}}, which runs north–south, while from the south along {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 179 / Tokyo Metropolitan Route 179|Q11427110}} one reaches the worship hall via multiple {{jalink|stairways|Q12511}} and torii gates. ; Subsidiary shrines * Inari Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q124483190}} – Founded on September 25, {{jalink|1937|Q24666}} at {{jalink|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|Q11658682}} at the wish of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|Q5694703}}. It was established to enshrine the spirits of fallen aviation personnel who had devoted their lives to the development of national aviation but were not enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. In 1938, with the founding of the {{jalink|Army Aviation School|Q2822344}}, it was transferred from Tokorozawa to Toyooka (present-day {{jalink|Iruma Air Base|Q3015843}}). On September 3, 1945, before the site was requisitioned by U.S. forces, it was relocated to the grounds of this shrine. * {{jalink|Ayako Tenmangū|Q17191862|Ayako Tenjinsha}} – Enshrines {{jalink|Tajihi no Ayako}}, the {{jalink|wet nurse|Q472898}} of {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. * Kotesashi Shrine – Enshrines the war dead (heroic spirits) of the Kotesashi area. Kitano Tenjin Inari.jpg|Inari Shrine Kitano Tenjin Yagumo.jpg|Yakumo Shrine KitanoTenjin AyakoJinja.jpg|Ayako Tenjinsha Kotesashi-jinja 14.jpg|Kotesashi Shrine / Aviation Shrine ; Trees on the grounds * Mikoto-zakura – A {{jalink|cherry tree|Q19939}} said to have been planted by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} in front of the shrine; it has repeatedly died and regenerated through {{jalink|suckers|Q960522}}. * Dainagon Plum – Planted by {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}} in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 18 ({{jalink|1590|Q6764}}); the current tree (as of 2015) is the second generation. Claiming descent from Sugawara no Michizane and using the {{jalink|plum crest}}, Maeda Toshiie is said to have stopped at the then-dilapidated shrine during the {{jalink|Odawara Campaign|Q2622890}} and rebuilt the shrine buildings, planting a single plum tree. The name derives from his court rank of {{jalink|Dainagon|Q1354706}} at the time. ; Other features * Kitano Place-Name Origin Monument – A stone monument erected on September 25, {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 18 ({{jalink|2006|Q2021}}), recording that although recent land readjustment changed the shrine’s address to Kotesashi Motomachi, the surrounding place-name “Kitano” derives from this shrine. * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} encampment site * {{jalink|Temizuya}} – Rebuilt in recent years ({{jalink|2010s|Q19022}}), with a {{jalink|dragon|Q11675}} {{jalink|ceiling painting}}. * Kagura hall * {{jalink|Chikaraishi|Q1660393}} * {{jalink|Sacred horse|Q11591047}} (shinme) Dainagon-ume Kitano-Tenjin-sha.jpg|Dainagon Plum Kitano Tenjin shinme.jpg|Sacred horse === Cultural properties === === Saitama Prefecture–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Kitano Tenjin Engi{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/kenshiteibunkazai/bunzai_20100510170056823.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Engi, with seven former roller ends |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Painting) – Seven hanging scrolls on silk with color, with seven former roller ends === Tokorozawa City–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Monument inscribed with a poem on the Mikoto-zakura{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/meisyo/bunzai_20100601150043467.html |title=Poem Monument of the Mikoto-zakura |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Place of scenic beauty) – Erected in {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 2 ({{jalink|1802|Q6883}}); the oldest poem monument in the city. ** Medieval documents of Kitano Tenjinsha{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/komonjo/bunzai_20100810104554996.html |title=Medieval Documents of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Ancient documents) – Eleven documents including a land donation charter by {{jalink|Ashikaga Ujimitsu|Q4306934}}. === Affiliated shrines === * {{jalink|Fujimori Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Fujimori Inari Shrine}} * {{jalink|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Rokusho Shrine}} * {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Suwa Shrine}} – {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}} Kotesashi Minami 1-17-21 Suwa Jinja Kotesashi.jpg|Suwa Shrine === Annual festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – Prayer amulets are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsumōde|Q1032381}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * {{jalink|Setsubun Festival}} ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – Corresponds to the shrine’s Great Purification; “Shikinaisha Kitano Shrine Great Purification Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsuuma|Q510847}} Festival ({{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}) – Held at the Inari Shrine; “Inari Shrine Amulets” are distributed. * Prayer for Harvest Festival ({{jalink|February 27|Q2361}}) – Also called Toshigoi-no-matsuri; “Prayer for Harvest Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|March 21|Q2433}}) – Paired with the Autumn Grand Festival and regarded as the most important festival of the shrine; “Kuniichi Chigi Shrine Good Fortune and Protection Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Festival ({{jalink|March 25|Q2454}}) – Also called Niban-machi; “Mononobe Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Sericulture|Q11236}} Festival ({{jalink|April 3|Q2509}}) – Held at the Ayako Tenjinsha; “Ayako Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * Memorial Service ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) – Held at the Kotesashi Shrine to console the spirits of local war dead since the Sino-Japanese and Russo-Japanese wars. * Yakumo Festival – Eve festival on {{jalink|July 14|Q2691}}, main festival on {{jalink|July 15|Q2686}}; “Yakumo Shrine Disaster-Prevention Amulets” are distributed. * Autumn Grand Festival (late September) – Hayashi music is offered every year, and Satokagura is offered every two years. * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) – “Niiname Festival Amulets” are distributed.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kitanotenjin.com/nenkangyouji.html |title=Festivals of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Kitano Tenjinsha |accessdate=2022-01-15}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Tokorozawa City History Research, No. 3: “On the Kitano Tenjin Engi Picture Scrolls” (Author: Nakamichi Kurihara) === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District Part 3|chapter=Kitano Village, Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} * [https://kitanotenjin.com/ Kitano Tenjinsha] (official shrine website) * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha (Kitano Tenjinsha) |url=https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8910/ |publisher=Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2018-03-12}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Kitano Tenjinsha |url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |publisher=Tokorozawa City |date=2015-06-23 |accessdate=2017-10-15}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]

content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja
/ Tenman Tenjinsha |Image = [[File:Kitanoten-jinja (Tokorozawa), haiden.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}} 3-28-44 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|47|26.0|N|139|25|43.3|E|region:JP-11_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kitano Tenjinsha}} |Deities = {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (Minor Shrine 2 / Minor Shrine 1 {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}})
{{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Founded = Unknown |Main hall = |Alternate name = Kitano Tenjinsha (collective name) |Annual festival = {{jalink|March 21|Q2433}} (Spring Grand Festival) |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Saitama|label=Kitano Tenjinsha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=210|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=210}} }} [[File:Kitano-Tenjin-sha sandō.jpg|thumb|At the southern approach of Kitano Tenjinsha]] '''Kitano Tenjinsha''' (きたのてんじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}}{{jalink|Kotesashi Motomachi|Q11460790}}. It is a {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}} (two proposed identifications, one {{jalink|disputed shrine|Q135022904}}), and under the {{jalink|modern system of shrine rankings|Q712534|former shrine ranking system}} it was classified as a {{jalink|prefectural shrine}}. The registered {{jalink|corporate registry|Q83708009}} religious corporation name is '''Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha''', and “Kitano Tenjinsha” is the collective name for these shrines.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha (Kitano) |date=2008-10-31 |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} === Enshrined deities === * Mononobe Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Kushitama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “Mononobe Tenjinsha”; jointly enshrines {{jalink|Izumo Iwai Shrine (disambiguation)|Q104539298|Izumo Iwai Shrine}}. * Kuniichi Chigi Jinja: {{jalink|Ōkuninushi|Q276944|Yachihoko-no-Kami}} – identified with the minor Shikinaisha “{{jalink|Kuniichi Chigi Shrine (disambiguation)||Kuniichi Chigi Shrine}}”. * Tenman Tenjinsha: {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}} === Jointly enshrined === * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} * Kotesashi Myōjin * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hohi|Q10940685|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} * {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}Summary displayed in front of the shrine, Kitano Tenjinsha. Viewed January 15, 2022. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded when {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} established the shrine during his eastern campaign. During the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it received a {{jalink|shuinjō|Q11093673}} from the {{jalink|Tokugawa shogunate|Q205662}}. * Mononobe Tenjinsha was the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} (clan tutelary deity) of the Mononobe clan (see {{jalink|Nashiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko#Clan|Q11504612}}). The ancestral deity {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto}} is still enshrined today. * As a Tenjin shrine, in {{jalink|Chōtoku|Q1327213}} 1 ({{jalink|995|Q23794}}), {{jalink|Sugawara no Sukenari}}, a fifth-generation descendant of Sugawara no Michizane, invited the deity from Kyoto’s {{jalink|Kitano Tenmangū|Q662176|Kitano Shrine}} while serving as {{jalink|Governor of Musashi}}. Thereafter, shrine buildings were constructed by {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}}, {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800}}, {{jalink|Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107}}, and {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}}. * Edo public exhibition – From April 1 to June 1, {{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 7 ({{jalink|1722|Q6918}}), a public display of the shrine’s sacred object was held at the former {{jalink|Mejiro Fudō-in Hasedera|Q11646320}} in {{jalink|Edo|Q215646}}. During this period, on April 24, hanging scrolls and documents from the shrine treasury were submitted inside Edo Castle at the shogun’s request.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hm9k-ajm/musasinorekisi/gosikifudoutomusasino/mejirofudou/kitanotennjinndekaityou/kitanotennjinnsyadekaityou.htm |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Edo Public Exhibition |work=Otamajakushi |date=2001-11-11 |publisher=Kiichi Ajima |accessdate=2015-06}} === Shrine grounds === Approaches lined with {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates and {{jalink|stone lanterns}} run along the western and southern sides of the grounds. The western side faces the former {{jalink|Kamakura Highway|Q2389309}}, which runs north–south, while from the south along {{jalink|Saitama Prefectural Route 179 / Tokyo Metropolitan Route 179|Q11427110}} one reaches the worship hall via multiple {{jalink|stairways|Q12511}} and torii gates. ; Subsidiary shrines * Inari Shrine * Yakumo Shrine * {{jalink|Aviation Shrine (Tokorozawa)|Q124483190}} – Founded on September 25, {{jalink|1937|Q24666}} at {{jalink|Tokorozawa Army Airfield|Q11658682}} at the wish of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshitoshi|Q5694703}}. It was established to enshrine the spirits of fallen aviation personnel who had devoted their lives to the development of national aviation but were not enshrined at Yasukuni Shrine. In 1938, with the founding of the {{jalink|Army Aviation School|Q2822344}}, it was transferred from Tokorozawa to Toyooka (present-day {{jalink|Iruma Air Base|Q3015843}}). On September 3, 1945, before the site was requisitioned by U.S. forces, it was relocated to the grounds of this shrine. * {{jalink|Ayako Tenmangū|Q17191862|Ayako Tenjinsha}} – Enshrines {{jalink|Tajihi no Ayako}}, the {{jalink|wet nurse|Q472898}} of {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. * Kotesashi Shrine – Enshrines the war dead (heroic spirits) of the Kotesashi area. Kitano Tenjin Inari.jpg|Inari Shrine Kitano Tenjin Yagumo.jpg|Yakumo Shrine KitanoTenjin AyakoJinja.jpg|Ayako Tenjinsha Kotesashi-jinja 14.jpg|Kotesashi Shrine / Aviation Shrine ; Trees on the grounds * Mikoto-zakura – A {{jalink|cherry tree|Q19939}} said to have been planted by {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258}} in front of the shrine; it has repeatedly died and regenerated through {{jalink|suckers|Q960522}}. * Dainagon Plum – Planted by {{jalink|Maeda Toshiie|Q1196817}} in {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} 18 ({{jalink|1590|Q6764}}); the current tree (as of 2015) is the second generation. Claiming descent from Sugawara no Michizane and using the {{jalink|plum crest}}, Maeda Toshiie is said to have stopped at the then-dilapidated shrine during the {{jalink|Odawara Campaign|Q2622890}} and rebuilt the shrine buildings, planting a single plum tree. The name derives from his court rank of {{jalink|Dainagon|Q1354706}} at the time. ; Other features * Kitano Place-Name Origin Monument – A stone monument erected on September 25, {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}} 18 ({{jalink|2006|Q2021}}), recording that although recent land readjustment changed the shrine’s address to Kotesashi Motomachi, the surrounding place-name “Kitano” derives from this shrine. * {{jalink|Prince Muneyoshi|Q541057}} encampment site * {{jalink|Temizuya}} – Rebuilt in recent years ({{jalink|2010s|Q19022}}), with a {{jalink|dragon|Q11675}} {{jalink|ceiling painting}}. * Kagura hall * {{jalink|Chikaraishi|Q1660393}} * {{jalink|Sacred horse|Q11591047}} (shinme) Dainagon-ume Kitano-Tenjin-sha.jpg|Dainagon Plum Kitano Tenjin shinme.jpg|Sacred horse === Cultural properties === === Saitama Prefecture–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Kitano Tenjin Engi{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/kenshiteibunkazai/bunzai_20100510170056823.html |title=Kitano Tenjinsha Engi, with seven former roller ends |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Painting) – Seven hanging scrolls on silk with color, with seven former roller ends === Tokorozawa City–designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Monument inscribed with a poem on the Mikoto-zakura{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/meisyo/bunzai_20100601150043467.html |title=Poem Monument of the Mikoto-zakura |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Place of scenic beauty) – Erected in {{jalink|Kyōwa|Q960763}} 2 ({{jalink|1802|Q6883}}); the oldest poem monument in the city. ** Medieval documents of Kitano Tenjinsha{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/bunkakyoyo/bunkazai/shishiteibunkazai/komonjo/bunzai_20100810104554996.html |title=Medieval Documents of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Tokorozawa City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Protection Division |accessdate=2015-06}} (Ancient documents) – Eleven documents including a land donation charter by {{jalink|Ashikaga Ujimitsu|Q4306934}}. === Affiliated shrines === * {{jalink|Fujimori Inari Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Fujimori Inari Shrine}} * {{jalink|Rokusho Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Rokusho Shrine}} * {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokorozawa)||Suwa Shrine}} – {{jalink|Saitama Prefecture|Q128186}}{{jalink|Tokorozawa City|Q165857}} Kotesashi Minami 1-17-21 Suwa Jinja Kotesashi.jpg|Suwa Shrine === Annual festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) – Prayer amulets are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsumōde|Q1032381}} ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * {{jalink|Setsubun Festival}} ({{jalink|February 3|Q2315}}) – Corresponds to the shrine’s Great Purification; “Shikinaisha Kitano Shrine Great Purification Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Hatsuuma|Q510847}} Festival ({{jalink|February 11|Q2335}}) – Held at the Inari Shrine; “Inari Shrine Amulets” are distributed. * Prayer for Harvest Festival ({{jalink|February 27|Q2361}}) – Also called Toshigoi-no-matsuri; “Prayer for Harvest Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Grand Festival ({{jalink|March 21|Q2433}}) – Paired with the Autumn Grand Festival and regarded as the most important festival of the shrine; “Kuniichi Chigi Shrine Good Fortune and Protection Amulets” are distributed. * Spring Festival ({{jalink|March 25|Q2454}}) – Also called Niban-machi; “Mononobe Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * {{jalink|Sericulture|Q11236}} Festival ({{jalink|April 3|Q2509}}) – Held at the Ayako Tenjinsha; “Ayako Tenjinsha Amulets” are distributed. * Memorial Service ({{jalink|April 15|Q2497}}) – Held at the Kotesashi Shrine to console the spirits of local war dead since the Sino-Japanese and Russo-Japanese wars. * Yakumo Festival – Eve festival on {{jalink|July 14|Q2691}}, main festival on {{jalink|July 15|Q2686}}; “Yakumo Shrine Disaster-Prevention Amulets” are distributed. * Autumn Grand Festival (late September) – Hayashi music is offered every year, and Satokagura is offered every two years. * {{jalink|Niiname Festival|Q11501518}} ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) – “Niiname Festival Amulets” are distributed.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://kitanotenjin.com/nenkangyouji.html |title=Festivals of Kitano Tenjinsha |publisher=Kitano Tenjinsha |accessdate=2022-01-15}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Tokorozawa City History Research, No. 3: “On the Kitano Tenjin Engi Picture Scrolls” (Author: Nakamichi Kurihara) === Related literature === * {{cite book|Japanese|title={{jalink|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 158, Iruma District Part 3|chapter=Kitano Village, Tenjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|764001/72}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Home Ministry|date=1884-6}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kitano-Tenjin-sha, Tokorozawa}} * [https://kitanotenjin.com/ Kitano Tenjinsha] (official shrine website) * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Mononobe Tenjinsha / Kuniichi Chigi Jinja / Tenman Tenjinsha (Kitano Tenjinsha) |url=https://www.saitama-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/8910/ |publisher=Saitama Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2018-03-12}} * {{Cite web|Japanese|title=Kitano Tenjinsha |url=https://www.city.tokorozawa.saitama.jp/iitokoro/enjoy/kids/shokai/meisyokyuseki/kitanotenjinsya.html |publisher=Tokorozawa City |date=2015-06-23 |accessdate=2017-10-15}} == Categories == [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tokorozawa]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tokorozawa, Saitama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]
